display.label: Water Supply — Household Coverage (BSWS & Safely Managed)

dqc_rules.message: BSWS Level I coverage exceeds 100% of projected households
dqc_rules.hint: Household coverage should not exceed the projected number of households. Over 100% usually means the count or the household base in the source file is off — check and re-upload.

dqc_rules.hint: Household coverage should not exceed the projected number of households. Over 100% usually means the count or the household base in the source file is off — check and re-upload.

dqc_rules.hint: Household coverage should not exceed the projected number of households. Over 100% usually means the count or the household base in the source file is off — check and re-upload.

dqc_rules.message: BSWS Total coverage exceeds 100% of projected households
dqc_rules.hint: Total household water-supply coverage should not exceed the projected number of households. Over 100% usually means a count or the household base in the source file is off — check and re-upload.

dqc_rules.message: Safely Managed Drinking Water Services coverage exceeds 100% of projected households
dqc_rules.hint: Coverage should not exceed the projected number of households. Over 100% usually means the count or the household base in the source file is off — check and re-upload.

notes: WASH water-supply file (envi_water_nir.xlsx), municipality-level (68 sheet rows: region + 3 province subtotals + 63 LGUs; the region row is skipped on ingest and recomputed by the dashboard, matching every other template). Quarterly only — no Annual/monthly sheets; the annual view is computed from stored quarters. psgc in col 0, location NAME in col 1 (not col 2 — this file has no 'NIRA' filler column, unlike the Infectious Disease files). No age/sex disaggregation and no sensitive indicators (household infrastructure counts). Percentages divide by Projected Number of Households (col 2). DQC: five over-100% coverage flags (the source sheet's own conditional formatting flags >100%). DEFERRED: the source file also carries a cross-column consistency check 'Level I + Level III >= Safely Managed Drinking Water Services' (col 13, DQC text only, not a stored value) — this sum-vs-value reconciliation needs a new DQC rule type (consolidated summary decision D4) that doesn't exist yet, so it is intentionally not implemented here. The sibling sanitation file (envi_sanitation_zod_nir.xlsx) is a separate, later build: its Qtr3/Qtr4 have a stray extra column A that shifts all indices +1, a DOH-side source fix (see wash.md FLAG S-1).
